cmd_manager:
🐧 Linux Command & Workflow Manager (cmd_manager) 🐧

Purpose: 
A hierarchical, searchable knowledge base and template engine for Linux commands, Docker workflows, and DevOps tasks.

Key Features:

Nested Categories: 
Uses a tree-view to organize commands into folders and sub-folders (e.g., Docker > Traefik).

Editable Templates: 
Allows you to modify variables (like IPs or tokens) in a text box before hitting "Copy to Clipboard", leaving the original template intact.

Smart Search: 
Filters the tree dynamically by matching keywords against both the command code and the description.

Context Menu: 
Right-click anywhere to add, rename, or delete groups and command elements.

Storage: 
Saves all data locally to a commands.json file.


cmd_manager  [Root]
├── core  [Folder]
│   ├── __pycache__  [Folder]
│   │   ├── __init__.cpython-313.pyc  [.PYC]

│   └── database.py  [.PY]
├── data  [Folder]
│   └── commands.json  [.JSON]
├── gui  [Folder]
│   ├── __pycache__  [Folder]

│   │   ├── context_menu.cpython-313.pyc  [.PYC]

│   └── main_window.py  [.PY]
└── main.py  [.PY]
ansible_doc_manager:
⚙️Ansible Playbook Documentation Manager (ansible_doc_manager)⚙

Purpose: 
A specialized, localized wiki for documenting Infrastructure as Code, specifically tailored for Ansible Playbooks and their resulting infrastructure.

Key Features:

Playbook Templating: Z
Right-clicking and selecting "Add Playbook Template" instantly generates a parent folder with pre-formatted Description and Repo Structure markdown files.

Seamless Editing: 
Features a single-panel text editor with background auto-saving (when clicking away) and a Ctrl+S hotkey so you never lose your documentation progress.

Mass Export: 
Allows multi-selection (Ctrl+Click) of playbook folders to instantly extract and stitch all their child documents into nicely formatted .txt files for easy sharing.

Storage: 
Saves all documentation to a playbooks.json file.

script_launcher:
🚀 Python App Launcher & Control Center (script_launcher)🚀

Purpose:
A centralized, flat-list launchpad to easily find, document, and execute all your custom Python scripts and modular GUI applications.
Key Features:

Smart Detection: 
Scans a hardcoded base folder. It smartly identifies "Modular Apps" (folders containing a main.py) and "Standalone Scripts" (single .py files), hiding all backend clutter like core/, gui/, and __pycache__.

Execution Engine: 
Double-clicking an app automatically detects your OS (Windows, Mac, Linux) and spawns a brand new terminal window to run the script.

CLI Arguments: 
Includes an input box to pass arguments (like --debug) to your scripts before launching.

Built-in Documentation: 
Features a split-pane design. Single-clicking any app loads its specific documentation/notes on the right side, which can be edited and saved via Ctrl+S.

Storage: 
Keeps track of app notes in a script_descriptions.json file.
